2606.16934.title: Exploring Extrinsic and Intrinsic Properties for Effective Reasoning with Code Interpreter
2606.16934.paper_summary: Reasoning with a Code Interpreter (CI) has emerged as an effective paradigm for enhancing the reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs) through executable computation and iterative verification. Despite its growing adoption, the behavioral properties underlying effective code reasoning remain largely underexplored. In this work, we investigate code reasoning from two distinct perspectives inspired by prior studies of natural language reasoning: extrinsic properties, represented by crucial tokens, and intrinsic properties, represented by code-specific cognitive behaviors. Across multiple LLMs, we find that stronger CI reasoning models consistently exhibit a higher prevalence of crucial tokens and cognitive behaviors, particularly verification, backtracking, and backward chaining. Building on these observations, we examine how these properties can be leveraged during both inference and training. At inference time, appending code-specific crucial tokens improves performance on several reasoning capabilities, including mathematical, ordering, and optimization, while yielding limited benefits elsewhere. At training time, augmenting a state-of-the-art framework with code-specific cognitive behaviors improves supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning performance in two of three evaluated models. Further analysis shows that these behaviors reduce overthinking in incorrect responses and improve token efficiency, while also revealing factors that limit gains in a certain model. Our findings provide the first systematic characterization of effective reasoning with CI and demonstrate both the potential and limitations of leveraging key properties to improve CI-based reasoning.
2606.16934.paper_summary_zh: 推理與代碼解釋器（CI）已成為通過可執行計算和迭代驗證來增強大型語言模型（LLMs）推理能力的有效範式。<lb/>儘管其採用日益增長，但有效代碼推理的行為特性仍然在很大程度上未被深入探討。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們從兩個不同的角度研究代碼推理，這些角度受到自然語言推理先前研究的啟發：外在特性，由關鍵標記表示，和內在特性，由代碼特定的認知行為表示。<lb/>在多個LLMs中，我們發現更強的CI推理模型始終表現出更高的關鍵標記和認知行為的普遍性，特別是驗證、回溯和反向鏈接。<lb/>基於這些觀察，我們檢查了如何在推理和訓練過程中利用這些特性。<lb/>在推理時，附加代碼特定的關鍵標記提高了在數學、排序和優化等多個推理能力上的表現，同時在其他方面的收益有限。<lb/>在訓練時，將最先進的框架增強為代碼特定的認知行為，改善了三個評估模型中兩個的監督微調和強化學習表現。<lb/>進一步分析顯示，這些行為減少了對錯誤回應的過度思考並提高了標記效率，同時也揭示了限制某一模型收益的因素。<lb/>我們的研究結果提供了有效使用CI進行推理的首個系統性特徵描述，並展示了利用關鍵特性改善基於CI的推理的潛力和限制。
2606.16934.author: Patomporn Payoungkhamdee et.al.
2606.16934.authors: Patomporn Payoungkhamdee, Napat Laosaengpha, Jenta Wonglertsakul, Pittawat Taveekitworachai, Pume Tuchinda, Panjapong Poobanchuen, Ekapol Chuangsuwanich, Can Udomcharoenchaikit, Samuel Cahyawijaya, Peerat Limkonchotiwat, Sarana Nutanong
